article_title: Artemis II Crew Arrives at Kennedy Space Center as Countdown Begins for First Crewed Lunar Voyage in 53 Years

content_preview.title: Artemis II Crew Arrives at Kennedy Space Center as Countdown Begins for First Crewed Lunar Voyage in 53 Years
content_preview.summary: Four astronauts reached Florida on March 27 ahead of an April 1 launch that will send humans beyond Earth orbit for the first time since Apollo 17.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The four astronauts assigned to NASA's Artemis II mission arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida on March 27, marking the final major pre-launch milestone before what is set to become the first crewed voyage beyond Earth orbit since December 1972. Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, and Mission Specialists Christina Koch and Jeremy Hansen flew from Johnson Space Center in Houston aboard T-38 training jets, [touching down at the spaceport's Launch and Landing Facility ar...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent space coverage with historic firsts.
editor_notes.source_verification: Spaceflight Now confirmed crew arrival March 27, April 1 launch, ten-day mission, first lunar since Apollo 17.
